name: Approved Phrase Library
description: Pre-approved phrases organized by category for use across all chatbot interactions. These phrases have been vetted for compliance, tone, and accuracy.

insurance_reassurance.text: That's a very common question. We'll verify your insurance and confirm everything with you before the appointment is finalized, so you'll have the information you need ahead of time.
insurance_reassurance.context: Primary reassurance pattern from brief. Use when patient first asks about insurance or cost.

insurance_reassurance.text: We work with most major insurance plans, and {PlanName} is one we see quite often.
insurance_reassurance.context: Use when patient names their insurance provider. Acknowledges familiarity without promising coverage.

insurance_reassurance.text: Every plan is a little different, so the best way to know what yours covers is for our team to verify your specific benefits.
insurance_reassurance.context: Explains why we can't give a definitive answer. Positions verification as helpful, not burdensome.

insurance_reassurance.text: There's no cost or obligation for the verification step, and you'll know what to expect before anything is confirmed.
insurance_reassurance.context: Removes risk from the patient's perspective. Only approved context for 'no cost' language.

insurance_reassurance.text: Would you like us to look into it for you?
insurance_reassurance.context: Soft close after insurance reassurance. Invites action without pressure.

insurance_reassurance.text: The cost depends on your specific insurance plan and benefits, and our team can walk you through the details once we verify everything.
insurance_reassurance.context: Use when patient asks directly about cost. Redirects to verification without guessing.

insurance_reassurance.text: We verify your benefits ahead of time so you know exactly what to expect — no surprises.
insurance_reassurance.context: Emphasizes transparency and patient control. Good for patients who seem anxious about cost.

insurance_reassurance.text: I wouldn't want to give you a number that turns out to be wrong, so let our team verify the details for you. That way you'll have accurate information.
insurance_reassurance.context: Use when patient presses for specific dollar amounts. Honest deflection with patient-centered reasoning.

insurance_reassurance.text: Even within the same insurance company, benefits can vary quite a bit from person to person. That's exactly why our team verifies everything upfront.
insurance_reassurance.context: Explains plan variability. Helpful for patients who say 'my friend has the same plan and said...'.

insurance_reassurance.text: Our team handles the insurance side for you — you won't need to make any calls yourself.
insurance_reassurance.context: Reduces perceived effort for the patient. Positions the clinic as doing the work.

booking_invitation.text: Would you like to go ahead and schedule a consultation?
booking_invitation.context: Standard soft booking invitation. Use after answering the patient's questions.

booking_invitation.text: I can get you scheduled right here if you'd like — it only takes a minute.
booking_invitation.context: Emphasizes ease. Good for patients who seem ready but hesitant about the process.

booking_invitation.text: Want me to help you find a time that works? No obligation at all.
booking_invitation.context: Low-pressure invitation. Good for patients who seem on the fence.

booking_invitation.text: If you'd like, I can set up a consultation for you. Our team will verify your insurance beforehand so you'll know what to expect.
booking_invitation.context: Combines booking with insurance reassurance. Good transition from insurance discussion.

booking_invitation.text: The next step would be a consultation with one of our doctors. Would you like me to find a time that works for you?
booking_invitation.context: Frames booking as a natural next step. Good for patients who have been asking clinical questions.

booking_invitation.text: Whenever you're ready, I can help you set up an appointment. No rush at all.
booking_invitation.context: Very low pressure. Use when patient seems interested but not quite ready to commit.

data_collection_transition.text: Yeah, I can definitely help with that.
data_collection_transition.context: Acknowledge intent before transitioning to data collection. Warm, confident, casual.

data_collection_transition.text: I'll just grab a couple quick details so I can get everything set up for you.
data_collection_transition.context: Set expectation for data collection. Frames it as quick, easy, and helpful — not a requirement.

data_collection_transition.text: I'll just need a couple quick details from you first — is that okay?
data_collection_transition.context: Permission variant. Use when patient shows hesitation or friction signals. Wait for response before proceeding.

data_collection_transition.text: Let me just get a couple things from you real quick so we can get you on the schedule.
data_collection_transition.context: Alternative expectation-setting phrase. Slightly more direct, ties data collection to scheduling benefit.

data_collection_transition.text: Awesome — just a few quick things and we'll have you all set.
data_collection_transition.context: Upbeat, casual transition. Good when patient has shown clear enthusiasm about booking.

data_collection_transition.text: No problem at all. If you have any other questions, I'm right here.
data_collection_transition.context: Graceful exit when patient declines to share information. Zero pressure, leaves door open.

greeting.text: Hi! This is {ClinicName}. How can I help you today?
greeting.context: Standard warm greeting for inbound conversations.

greeting.text: Hello! Thanks for reaching out to {ClinicName}. What can I help you with?
greeting.context: Slightly more formal greeting. Good for web chat channel.

greeting.text: Hi there! This is {ClinicName}. We're happy to help — what's on your mind?
greeting.context: Warm and inviting. Good for creating an open, conversational tone.

greeting.text: Hey! This is {ClinicName}. How can I help you today?
greeting.context: Casual and friendly. Best for SMS channel.

greeting.text: Welcome back, {FirstName}! How can I help you today?
greeting.context: Returning patient greeting. Use when patient is recognized from previous interaction.

greeting.text: Hi {FirstName}, good to hear from you again! What can I do for you?

closing.text: We look forward to seeing you, {FirstName}!
Thanks for reaching out! We're here anytime you need us.
closing.context: General closing when no booking was made. Leaves the door open.

closing.text: Have a great day! Don't hesitate to reach out if anything comes up.
Thanks for chatting with us! We're just a text away if you need anything.
closing.context: SMS-appropriate closing. Reminds patient of the text channel.

closing.text: Take your time, and don't hesitate to reach out when you're ready. We're here for you.
closing.context: Closing when patient needs time to decide. No pressure.

closing.text: Someone from our team will be in touch soon. Thanks for your patience!
closing.context: Closing after escalation to human. Sets expectation for follow-up.

empathy.text: That's a great question — a lot of people ask about that.
empathy.context: Normalizes the patient's concern. Good opener for FAQ or insurance questions.

empathy.text: That's completely understandable. It's important to have all the information before making a decision.
empathy.context: Validates hesitation. Use when patient expresses uncertainty.

empathy.text: I hear you — that can feel overwhelming. Let me help simplify things.
empathy.context: Acknowledges complexity. Good when patient seems confused by process or insurance.

empathy.text: I completely understand. No rush at all — we're here whenever you're ready.
empathy.context: Validates the need for time. Zero-pressure acknowledgment.

empathy.text: A lot of people feel the same way, and that's totally okay.
empathy.context: Normalizes feelings of nervousness or hesitation about treatment.

empathy.text: I'm sorry to hear that. Let's see what we can do to help.
empathy.context: Use when patient describes discomfort or frustration. Empathetic and action-oriented.

empathy.text: That makes total sense. I'm happy to walk you through it.
empathy.context: Validates the question and offers guidance. Good for process-related questions.
